satire_attempt: It's 2:47 AM. The on-call has stopped typing. The senior incident commander, channeling Krishna, intones: 'kutas tvā kaśmalam idam viṣame samupasthitam.' The on-call, because they did six months of Sanskrit at Stanford, immediately understands and runs step 4 of the rollback playbook. Production is restored. A book deal follows. Subscribe to the newsletter.
verdict_reason: My satire only works by inventing the implausible scene of an IC quoting verbatim Sanskrit during an outage — and the engineering layer does not actually claim the IC quotes Sanskrit. It claims the verse describes a scene the IC instinctively performs. The mockery requires inventing a claim the verse does not make. PASS.
